content.full_text:
February 22, 1978

Barbara Campbell, Esq.
LEGAL DEPARTMENT
Fair Political Practice Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Campbell:

It is my understanding that pursuant to a telephone conversation with Mr. Burns of San Diego Gas and Electric Company, it will not be necessary for my client to file any statement under Proposition 9 with the following facts.

San Diego Gas and Electric Company will make one or more lump sum payments to my client, an Advertising Agency. These funds will be used in turn by my client for lobbying activities such as advertising and public relations. San Diego Gas and Electric will fully report the payments made. Under these facts it will not be necessary for my client to file any statement pursuant to Proposition 9 as codified in the California Government Code. If you concur, please sign below where indicated and return this letter to me.

Thank you in advance for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

A. DAVID STUTZ
Attorney at Law

ADS:tlr
encls.

Concurred to:

Barbara Campbell
BARBARA CAMPBELL, ESQ.

sections.question_synthetic: Does a lobbying advertising agency that receives lump sum payments from San Diego Gas and Electric Company need to file a statement under Proposition 9 (California's Political Reform Act) if the utility company fully reports the payments?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concurred that the advertising agency does not need to file a statement under Proposition 9 when receiving lump sum payments from San Diego Gas and Electric Company for lobbying activities, provided the utility company fully reports the payments made.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter signed for concurrence by FPPC. No formal question/conclusion sections present; question and conclusion derived from letter content. FPPC response indicated by signature of concurrence by Barbara Campbell, ESQ.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a lobbying advertising agency that receives lump sum payments from San Diego Gas and Electric Company need to file a statement under Proposition 9 (California's Political Reform Act) if the utility company fully reports the payments?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC concurred that the advertising agency does not need to file a statement under Proposition 9 when receiving lump sum payments from San Diego Gas and Electric Company for lobbying activities, provided the utility company fully reports the payments made.

embedding.summary: An attorney requested FPPC confirmation that his client, an advertising agency receiving lump sum payments from San Diego Gas and Electric Company for lobbying activities, would not need to file a Proposition 9 statement if the utility company fully reported the payments. The FPPC concurred with this interpretation.
